deep_dive.title: TRIDENT: Breaking the Hybrid-Safety-Physics Coupling for Provably Safe Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
deep_dive.abstract: Safe coordination in networked cyber-physical systems forces learning algorithms to simultaneously handle hybrid discrete-continuous actions, hard training-time safety constraints, and physics-governed dynamics. We show that these three features form a directed cycle of biases that defeats any naive composition of off-the-shelf modules, and formalize this as a three-way coupling lemma. We then introduce TRIDENT, the first MARL framework whose three components are co-designed to cancel each leak: a Richardson-Romberg gradient correction reducing Gumbel-Softmax bias from O(tau) to O(tau^2), a Lyapunov-constrained sequential trust-region update enforcing per-iterate feasibility, and a physics-informed residual critic that decomposes value rather than reward. We prove an O~(1/sqrt(K)) convergence rate to a constrained Nash equilibrium and an O(sqrt(K)) cumulative-violation bound. On multi-UAV mobile-edge computing, autonomous intersection management, and a hybrid SMAC variant, TRIDENT cuts training-time violations by 95.5% over MADDPG and 76.3% over MACPO, while improving reward by 13.5% over the strongest unconstrained baseline.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Provides provably safe MARL using Lyapunov constraints, closely related to CBF concept
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 使用Lyapunov约束实现可证明安全的多智能体RL，与控制屏障函数概念紧密相关
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 使用Lyapunov约束实现可证明安全的多智能体RL，与控制屏障函数概念紧密相关
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 使用Lyapunov约束实现可证明安全的多智能体RL，与控制屏障函数概念紧密相关
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces TRIDENT, a MARL framework co-designing gradient correction, Lyapunov-constrained trust-region updates, and physics-informed critic for provably safe coordination.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 多智能体安全强化学习面临混合动作、硬安全约束与物理动力学的耦合难题。本文提出TRIDENT框架，通过Richardson-Romberg梯度校正、李雅普诺夫约束序贯置信域更新和物理信息残差评论家协同设计，打破三方耦合，实现每步可行性保证。在多种多智能体系统中验证了安全性与性能。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 多智能体安全强化学习面临混合动作、硬安全约束与物理动力学的耦合难题。本文提出TRIDENT框架，通过Richardson-Romberg梯度校正、李雅普诺夫约束序贯置信域更新和物理信息残差评论家协同设计，打破三方耦合，实现每步可行性保证。在多种多智能体系统中验证了安全性与性能。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: Safe reinforcement learning integrated with control barrier functions for autonomous systems

quick_skim.title: Distributed Safe Consensus Under Asymmetric Input and Time-Varying Output Constraints
quick_skim.abstract: This paper studies safe distributed consensus for single-integrator multi-agent systems over connected undirected graphs under simultaneous asymmetric actuator constraints and output safety constraints. Each agent is equipped with a continuously differentiable asymmetric actuator dynamics that maps a commanded control signal to the realized plant input while keeping the latter strictly inside a prescribed admissible interval. To address output safety, a barrier-coordinate transformation is introduced over a common time-varying safe interval, and a distributed synchronization law is designed in the transformed coordinates. The resulting controller integrates a graph-based coordination layer with an actuator-side tracking layer, thereby enabling simultaneous enforcement of input admissibility, forward invariance of the safe output set, and asymptotic synchronization. For compact admissible sets of initial conditions, it is shown that the closed-loop solution is complete, all signals remain bounded, the actuator inputs remain strictly within their asymmetric bounds, and the agent outputs remain inside the prescribed safe interval for all time. Moreover, the transformed synchronization errors converge exponentially to zero, and the original agent outputs asymptotically synchronize to a designer-selected admissible trajectory embedded in the common safe interval. Numerical simulations validate the proposed framework and demonstrate safe consensus under both asymmetric actuation bounds and time-varying output constraints.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: distributed safe consensus using barrier-coordinate transformation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用障碍坐标变换的分布式安全共识
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用障碍坐标变换的分布式安全共识
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用障碍坐标变换的分布式安全共识
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a distributed safe consensus controller using barrier-coordinate transformation to handle asymmetric actuator constraints and time-varying output safety constraints.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对多智能体系统在非对称执行器和时变输出约束下的安全共识问题，引入障碍坐标变换，将状态约束转化为无约束系统，设计分布式同步律，实现同时满足执行器输入约束和输出安全约束的分布式控制。

quick_skim.title: Environment-Aware Stable Neural Koopman Dynamics Learning for Input-Driven Systems under Environmental Constraints
quick_skim.abstract: Constructing predictive models of nonlinear dynamical systems from measurement data is a longstanding problem in systems identification and control. Although Neural ordinary differential equations~(Neural ODEs), Koopman operator approximations, and input-aware architectures have each moved the field forward, none simultaneously addresses environment-varying operating conditions, rigorous stability guarantees, and input-to-state stability (ISS) certification within a unified trainable framework. This paper introduces Environment-Aware Stable Neural Koopman Dynamics Learning (ESNKD), which integrates four components: (i)~a bundle-structured encoder that maps environmental observations to a geometrically regularized latent manifold, drawing on the fiber bundle framework; (ii)~an input-conditioned Neural ODE whose residual term handles arbitrary external signals, extending the input concomitant philosophy; (iii)~a contraction synthesis layer enforcing convergence via Persidskii-type tractable linear inequalities, analogous to the certification mechanism; and (iv)~a Koopman lifting stage with LMI-based ISS verification that follows the theoretical pipeline of. Theoretical guarantees cover solution existence and uniqueness, incremental exponential stability, ISS with explicit gain bounds, and robustness to environmental perturbation. Experiments on five benchmark systems, including two robotic manipulation platforms, show consistent improvements over five competitive baselines in both prediction accuracy and safety certification rates.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Neural Koopman dynamics learning with stability and safety-relevant guarantees
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 具有稳定性与安全相关保证的神经Koopman动力学学习
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 具有稳定性与安全相关保证的神经Koopman动力学学习
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 具有稳定性与安全相关保证的神经Koopman动力学学习
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces Environment-Aware Stable Neural Koopman Dynamics Learning (ESNKD) that integrates environmental observations into a bundle-structured encoder and provides rigorous stability and input-to-state safety guarantees within a trainable framework.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对非线性动力系统建模中环境变化与稳定保证难以同时满足的问题，本文提出环境感知稳定神经Koopman动力学学习（ESNKD）方法，利用束结构编码器将环境观测映射到几何规则空间，并显式保证模型稳定性和输入到状态稳定性（ISS）。该方法可在统一可训练框架下提供形式化安全相关保证，适用于控制和安全验证。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对非线性动力系统建模中环境变化与稳定保证难以同时满足的问题，本文提出环境感知稳定神经Koopman动力学学习（ESNKD）方法，利用束结构编码器将环境观测映射到几何规则空间，并显式保证模型稳定性和输入到状态稳定性（ISS）。该方法可在统一可训练框架下提供形式化安全相关保证，适用于控制和安全验证。
